title: Three-arm benchmark with deterministic scoring to empirically measure skill/MCP value
context: fable-mode skill and Hunch MCP were built but lacked empirical measurement on this repo. Earlier findings showed uncertainty about the skill's marginal effect. Needed a scientific approach to validate whether these investments actually improve model performance on real tasks.
decision: Created A/B/C benchmark isolating three configurations (bare model, +fable-mode skill, +hunch MCP) with fresh detached worktrees per run, exact-string-revert bug re-introduction for fix tasks, and deterministic scoring: regex checklist for question tasks, hidden test-pass + no modification for fix tasks. Operator-level plugins/hooks excluded via --setting-sources project and --strict-mcp-config.
consequences: Empirical validation possible: can measure actual performance delta of skill and MCP on real repo work
High cost: 36 Opus sessions for full run (6 tasks × 3 arms × 2 reps)
Scoring measures fact coverage (regex hits, test pass), not prose quality — requires hand reading for confidence
Graph contains decisions about seeded bugs (validates product, not leakage, but must disclose when publishing results)
Results specific to this repo and task set — generalizability to other codebases unknown
alternatives_rejected: Live A/B testing on users (too expensive, risky to ship unvalidated)
Subjective manual code review (labor-intensive, not repeatable)
Single-worktree arms without isolation (confounding variables from prior state)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Live A/B testing on users (too expensive, risky to ship unvalidated)
Subjective manual code review (labor-intensive, not repeatable)
Single-worktree arms without isolation (confounding variables from prior state)
